title: RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED — Envelope consumption recorded
description: RUNTIME CONTRACT. Every payload published on the operational RabbitMQ topic exchange owned by CAP.BSP.004.ENV with routing key 'EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CONSUMED.RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED' MUST validate against this schema (ADR-TECH-STRAT-001 Rules 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). Per Rule 3, the payload is a *domain event DDD*: it carries the coherent atomic transition data of an envelope consumption — the data of the OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION aggregate state-change. It is NOT a snapshot of the read model RES.BSP.004.OPEN_ENVELOPE, and NOT a technical patch of fields. The available balance ('solde_disponible') is derivable on the consumer side via the formula 'solde_disponible = montant_plafond - montant_consomme' (post-transition); it is intentionally NOT a transported field — consumers compute it locally to keep the wire surface minimal and authoritative. Beneficiary identity resolution: the payload carries 'identifiant_dossier' as the CORRELATION KEY. Consumers wishing to obtain the canonical beneficiary identifier (OBJ.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_RECORD.identifiant_interne) MUST query CAP.REF.001.BEN with this 'identifiant_dossier'. The producer (CAP.BSP.004.ENV) NEVER carries the canonical 'identifiant_interne' on the wire (privacy + decoupling per ADR-BCM-URBA-0009).

identifiant_allocation.description: Unique identifier of the budget envelope allocation that has been consumed. Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.identifiant_allocation. Stable across replays; suitable for idempotency keys at the consumer side.

identifiant_dossier.description: CORRELATION KEY. Reference to the participation case (dossier) that owns this envelope. Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.identifiant_dossier. Identity resolution: consumers resolve 'identifiant_dossier' toward OBJ.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_RECORD.identifiant_interne via a CAP.REF.001.BEN (Beneficiary Reference) lookup. The producer (CAP.BSP.004.ENV) NEVER carries the canonical 'identifiant_interne' on the bus.

categorie.description: Spending category covered by this envelope (e.g. 'ALIMENTATION', 'TRANSPORT', 'SANTE', 'LOGEMENT'). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.categorie. The canonical authoritative source of the category set per tier is CAP.REF.001.PAL (Tier Catalogue). Free-form string at the contract level — no enum constraint here, to avoid coupling consumers to the current tier vocabulary; consumers needing an authoritative list MUST query CAP.REF.001.PAL.

montant_plafond.description: Maximum amount authorised for the envelope over the period (post-allocation, immutable for this allocation cycle). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.montant_plafond. Currency is implicit (system base currency); no multi-currency support in this version.

montant_consomme.description: Amount actually spent against the envelope AFTER the consumption transition (post-state of the aggregate). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.montant_consomme. Domain rule: 0 <= montant_consomme <= montant_plafond at the contract level — the producer guarantees post-transition coherence. Note: 'solde_disponible' is intentionally NOT carried; consumers derive it locally as 'montant_plafond - montant_consomme'.

periode_debut.description: Start date of the allocation period in ISO-8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.periode_debut.

periode_fin.description: End date of the allocation period in ISO-8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.periode_fin. Domain rule: 'periode_fin' >= 'periode_debut'.

timestamp_consommation.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the consumption transition was recorded by CAP.BSP.004.ENV. Domain-event-DDD-only field — anchors the transition in time independently of the period boundaries.
